nodes.x: Now concerning the collection for the saints, as I have given order to the churches of Galatia, even so do ye.

Tho Saints arc likewise informed, that we shall have constantly on hand for sale, at wholesale and retail, all the various works mentioned in the catalogue, published on the Inst pngc of tho Seei\ All those publications should bo in the houses of all tho Saints, and should be diligently read, that they may be well instructed in all the great principles of eternal salvation, that, through their…

also much of the history connected with the rise and progress of the Church. It is not expected that they will ever be reprinted, and when these copies are disposed of they cannot be obtained. I hope the Saints, as far as they can, will secure a copy of this work, that they may leave with their posterity an account of the rise and progress of the Church, which has been attended with so many…
And all the brethren which are with me, unto the churches of Galatia:
Upon the first day of the week let every one of you lay by him in store, as God hath prospered him, that there be no gatherings when I come.

We have met to commemorate the first day of the week, and we hope that every heart will be concentrated upon the business before us. We do not hold that the first day of the week is the only day upon which to worship God, for we ought also to worship him on the second, third, fourth, fifth, sixth, and seventh. It was the custom of Christ's disciples to meet together on the first day of the week…
The Lord God which gathereth the outcasts of Israel saith, Yet will I gather others to him, beside those that are gathered unto him.
But with him that standeth here with us this day before the Lord our God, and also with him that is not here with us this day:
And when I come, whomsoever ye shall approve by your letters, them will I send to bring your liberality unto Jerusalem.
¶ And it shall come to pass in that day, that I will seek to destroy all the nations that come against Jerusalem.
And this I have told you concerning Jerusalem; and when that day shall come, shall a remnant be scattered among all nations;
I make a decree, that all they of the people of Israel, and of his priests and Levites, in my realm, which are minded of their own freewill to go up to Jerusalem, go with thee.
And then shall they assist my people that they may be gathered in, who are scattered upon all the face of the land, in unto the New Jerusalem.
And they shall put my name upon the children of Israel; and I will bless them.
And if it be meet that I go also, they shall go with me.
And behold, ye shall meet together oft; and ye shall not forbid any man from coming unto you when ye shall meet together, but suffer them that they may come unto you and forbid them not;
Therefore now go, lead the people unto the place of which I have spoken unto thee: behold, mine Angel shall go before thee: nevertheless in the day when I visit I will visit their sin upon them.
Then shall they call upon me, but I will not answer; they shall seek me early, but they shall not find me:
And if I go and prepare a place for you, I will come again, and receive you unto myself; that where I am, there ye may be also.

"’I must be going,’ I said, looking at my watch. ’No doubt we shall meet
again before long.’
Now I will come unto you, when I shall pass through Macedonia: for I do pass through Macedonia.
And to pass by you into Macedonia, and to come again out of Macedonia unto you, and of you to be brought on my way toward Judæa.
Moreover, brethren, we do you to wit of the grace of God bestowed on the churches of Macedonia;
And indeed ye do it toward all the brethren which are in all Macedonia: but we beseech you, brethren, that ye increase more and more;
’Macedonian cry 'of “come over and help us’’ is so frequently, sounding in my ears, that I could not forbear, and have been influenced to labor, beyond my strength.
As I besought thee to abide still at Ephesus, when I went into Macedonia, that thou mightest charge some that they teach no other doctrine,
And it may be that I will abide, yea, and winter with you, that ye may bring me on my journey whithersoever I go.
Go your way whithersoever I will, and it shall be given you by the Comforter what you shall do and whither you shall go.

"If I should live and do well, I expect to come to Topsfield myself
next winter, which, if I do, I shall come and pay you a visit. Farewell.
For I will not see you now by the way; but I trust to tarry a while with you, if the Lord permit.
See, I will tarry in the plain of the wilderness, until there come word from you to certify me.
Now therefore, I pray you, tarry ye also here this night, that I may know what the Lord will say unto me more.
For I long to see you, that I may impart unto you some spiritual gift, to the end ye may be established;
Depart not hence, I pray thee, until I come unto thee, and bring forth my present, and set it before thee. And he said, I will tarry until thou come again.
But I will tarry at Ephesus until Pentecost.
For Paul had determined to sail by Ephesus, because he would not spend the time in Asia: for he hasted, if it were possible for him, to be at Jerusalem the day of Pentecost.
For yet a little while, and he that shall come will come, and will not tarry.
And Tychicus have I sent to Ephesus.
For a great door and effectual is opened unto me, and there are many adversaries.
Behold, and lo, I have much people in this place, in the regions round about; and an effectual door shall be opened in the regions round about in this eastern land.
Behold, I stand at the door, and knock: if any man hear my voice, and open the door, I will come in to him, and will sup with him, and he with me.
They also that render evil for good are mine adversaries; because I follow the thing that good is.
Now if Timotheus come, see that he may be with you without fear: for he worketh the work of the Lord, as I also do.

For this cause have I sent unto you Timotheus, who is my beloved son, and faithful in the Lord, who shall bring you into remembrance of my ways which be in Christ, as I teach every where in every church.
But now when Timotheus came from you unto us, and brought us good tidings of your faith and charity, and that ye have good remembrance of us always, desiring greatly to see us, as we also to see you:
But I trust in the Lord Jesus to send Timotheus shortly unto you, that I also may be of good comfort, when I know your state.
And sent Timotheus, our brother, and minister of God, and our fellowlabourer in the gospel of Christ, to establish you, and to comfort you concerning your faith:
Paul, and Silvanus, and Timotheus, unto the church of the Thessalonians in God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ:
Let no man therefore despise him: but conduct him forth in peace, that he may come unto me: for I look for him with the brethren.
These things speak, and exhort, and rebuke with all authority. Let no man despise thee.
He therefore that despiseth, despiseth not man, but God, who hath also given unto us his holy Spirit.

Wherefore, if I come, I will remember his deeds which he doeth, prating against us with malicious words: and not content therewith, neither doth he himself receive the brethren, and forbiddeth them that would, and casteth them out of the church.

said, “Sir, I make known to every man the 
great works of God: and I hope that all those 
who love them, and have sinned before, on 
hearing these words, may repent, and receive 
life again.” ‘Continue, therefore, in this min- 
istry, and finish it. And all who follow out his 
commands shall have life, and great honour with 
the Lord.t. But those who do not keep his 
commandments, flee from his…
As touching our brother Apollos, I greatly desired him to come unto you with the brethren: but his will was not at all to come at this time; but he will come when he shall have convenient time.

Therefore, O my son, whosoever will come may come and partake of the waters of life freely; and whosoever will not come the same is not compelled to come; but in the last day it shall be restored unto him according to his deeds.
Therefore, if ye shall come unto me, or shall desire to come unto me, and rememberest that thy brother hath aught against thee—
And when we were come to Jerusalem, the brethren received us gladly.
Watch ye, stand fast in the faith, quit you like men, be strong.
For now we live, if ye stand fast in the Lord.
Listen to the voice of Jesus Christ, your Lord, your God, and your Redeemer, whose word is quick and powerful.
Let all your things be done with charity.
And if you have not faith, hope, and charity, you can do nothing.
Let all things be done decently and in order.
And though I bestow all my goods to feed the poor, and though I give my body to be burned, and have not charity, it profiteth me nothing.
And above all things have fervent charity among yourselves: for charity shall cover the multitude of sins.
Open thy mouth, judge righteously, and plead the cause of the poor and needy.
I beseech you, brethren, (ye know the house of Stephanas, that it is the firstfruits of Achaia, and that they have addicted themselves to the ministry of the saints,)
Likewise greet the church that is in their house. Salute my wellbeloved Epænetus, who is the firstfruits of Achaia unto Christ.
That ye submit yourselves unto such, and to every one that helpeth with us, and laboureth.
And we beseech you, brethren, to know them which labour among you, and are over you in the Lord, and admonish you;

Or in other words, yield yourselves up unto us, and unite with us and become acquainted with our secret works, and become our brethren that ye may be like unto us—not our slaves, but our brethren and partners of all our substance.
Behold, I speak unto you, and also to all those who have desires to bring forth and establish this work;
I am glad of the coming of Stephanas and Fortunatus and Achaicus: for that which was lacking on your part they have supplied.
And the Arvadite, and the Zemarite, and the Hamathite.
I beseech you, brethren, (ye know the house of Stephanas, that it is the firstfruits of Achaia, and that they have addicted themselves to the ministry of the saints,)
And the sons of Elkanah; Amasai, and Ahimoth.
And Parmashta, and Arisai, and Aridai, and Vajezatha,
For they have refreshed my spirit and yours: therefore acknowledge ye them that are such.

And now my beloved brethren, I have spoken these words unto you according to the Spirit which testifieth in me; and my soul doth exceedingly rejoice, because of the exceeding diligence and heed which ye have given unto my word.
As also ye have acknowledged us in part, that we are your rejoicing, even as ye also are ours in the day of the Lord Jesus.

And now, my beloved brethren, for ye are my brethren, and ye ought to be beloved, and ye ought to bring forth works which are meet for repentance, seeing that your hearts have been grossly hardened against the word of God, and seeing that ye are a lost and a fallen people.

And now my beloved brethren, do you believe these things? Behold, I say unto you, yea, I know that ye believe them; and the way that I know that ye believe them is by the manifestation of the Spirit which is in me. And now because your faith is strong concerning that, yea, concerning the things which I have spoken, great is my joy.
The churches of Asia salute you. Aquila and Priscilla salute you much in the Lord, with the church that is in their house.
Salute one another with an holy kiss. The churches of Christ salute you.
Greet Priscilla and Aquila my helpers in Christ Jesus:
Salute Apelles approved in Christ. Salute them which are of Aristobulus’ household.
All that are with me salute thee. Greet them that love us in the faith. Grace be with you all. Amen.
All the brethren greet you. Greet ye one another with an holy kiss.
Greet all the brethren with an holy kiss.
Greet one another with an holy kiss.
Salute every saint in Christ Jesus. The brethren which are with me greet you.
The salutation of me Paul with mine own hand.
The salutation of Paul with mine own hand, which is the token in every epistle: so I write.
The salutation by the hand of me Paul. Remember my bonds. Grace be with you. Amen.

That your incomings may be in the name of the Lord, that your outgoings may be in the name of the Lord, that all your salutations may be in the name of the Lord, with uplifted hands unto the Most High—
I Paul have written it with mine own hand, I will repay it: albeit I do not say to thee how thou owest unto me even thine own self besides.
Then Agrippa said unto Paul, Thou art permitted to speak for thyself. Then Paul stretched forth the hand, and answered for himself:
If any man love not the Lord Jesus Christ, let him be Anathema Maran-atha.
But if any man love God, the same is known of him.

There is no way that I know of or have ever heard of, believed, or entertained any conception of, that will enable you any better to love God than to love man who is made in the image and likeness of God. Do you want to honor Him? Then honor man that is made in the likeness of God. “But,” says one, “some men are not good,” then honor those that are good, who are his ministers, in whom he is…
He that is faithful and endureth shall overcome the world.
The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you. Amen.
The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.
Grace to you, and peace, from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
Grace be to you and peace from God our Father, and from the Lord Jesus Christ.
Grace be to you, and peace, from God our Father, and from the Lord Jesus Christ.
My love be with you all in Christ Jesus. Amen.
The Lord Jesus Christ be with thy spirit. Grace be with you. Amen.
The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
